title: Douala International Airport

infobox.value: [Bafoussam](/wiki/Bafoussam_Airport "Bafoussam Airport"), [Bangui](/wiki/Bangui_M%27Poko_International_Airport "Bangui M'Poko International Airport")[[12]](#cite_note-camairnetwork-12), [Brazzaville](/wiki/Maya-Maya_Airport "Maya-Maya Airport")[[13]](#cite_note-camairroutes-13), [Cotonou](/wiki/Cadjehoun_Airport "Cadjehoun Airport")[[13]](#cite_note-camairroutes-13), [Libreville](/wiki/Libreville_International_Airport "Libreville International Airport")[[12]](#cite_note-camairnetwork-12), [Ngaoundéré](/wiki/Ngaound%C3%A9r%C3%A9_Airport "Ngaoundéré Airport")[[12]](#cite_note-camairnetwork-12), [Pointe-Noire](/wiki/Pointe_Noire_Airport "Pointe Noire Airport")[[13]](#cite_note-camairroutes-13), [Yaoundé](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9_Nsimalen_International_Airport "Yaoundé Nsimalen International Airport")[[12]](#cite_note-camairnetwork-12)  **Seasonal:** [Jeddah](/wiki/King_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "King Abdulaziz International Airport"), [Medina](/wiki/Prince_Mohammad_bin_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "Prince Mohammad bin Abdulaziz International Airport")[[14]](#cite_note-14)

(French: *Aéroport international de Douala*) ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **DLA**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **FKKD**) is an international airport located in [Douala](/wiki/Douala "Douala"), the largest city in [Cameroon](/wiki/Cameroon "Cameroon") and the capital of Cameroon's [Littoral Region](/wiki/Littoral_Region_(Cameroon) "Littoral Region (Cameroon)"). With its 4 terminals[[3]](#cite_note-3) and an average of 1.5 million passengers and 50,000 tonnes of freight per year,[[4]](#cite_note-4) it is the country's busiest airport. The airport is managed and partly owned (34%) by the company [Aeroport du Cameroon](/w/index.php?title=Aeroport_du_Cameroon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Aeroport du Cameroon (page does not exist)") (ADC) which also manages all other 13 airports on Cameroonian soil.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[Jeddah](/wiki/King_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "King Abdulaziz International Airport"), [Medina](/wiki/Prince_Mohammad_bin_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "Prince Mohammad bin Abdulaziz International Airport")[[14]](#cite_note-14) |

["Airport information for DLA"](http://www.gcmap.com/airport/DLA). *[DAFIF](/wiki/DAFIF "DAFIF")*. October 2006 – via Great Circle Mapper.

Archived from [the original](https://www.adcsa.aero/content/aeroport-international-douala) on 19 September 2018. Retrieved 18 September 2018.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/57/Asky_plane_7810.jpg/250px-Asky_plane_7810.jpg)](/wiki/File:Asky_plane_7810.jpg)

Douala International Airport with airplanes in the flight line

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/62/A%C3%A9roport_de_Douala_%28cameroun%29.jpg/250px-A%C3%A9roport_de_Douala_%28cameroun%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:A%C3%A9roport_de_Douala_(cameroun).jpg)

Front view of the Douala International Airport on a bright sunny day

**Douala International Airport** (French: *Aéroport international de Douala*) ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **DLA**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **FKKD**) is an international airport located in [Douala](/wiki/Douala "Douala"), the largest city in [Cameroon](/wiki/Cameroon "Cameroon") and the capital of Cameroon's [Littoral Region](/wiki/Littoral_Region_(Cameroon) "Littoral Region (Cameroon)"). With its 4 terminals[[3]](#cite_note-3) and an average of 1.5 million passengers and 50,000 tonnes of freight per year,[[4]](#cite_note-4) it is the country's busiest airport. The airport is managed and partly owned (34%) by the company [Aeroport du Cameroon](/w/index.php?title=Aeroport_du_Cameroon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Aeroport du Cameroon (page does not exist)") (ADC) which also manages all other 13 airports on Cameroonian soil.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Runway

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Douala_International_Airport&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Runway")]

Douala Airport has a single runway, 12/30, with a length of 2,880 m (9,448 ft). Between 1 and 21 March 2016, the runway was closed for upgrade works; all airlines switched operations to [Yaoundé Airport](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9_Nsimalen_International_Airport "Yaoundé Nsimalen International Airport") during that period.[[6]](#cite_note-6) This formed part of a renovation plan of 20 billion [CFA](/wiki/CFA_franc "CFA franc") ([US$](/wiki/United_States_dollar "United States dollar")36,363,636 million), financed by the French Agency of Development, which targeted a two-stage renovation: first the airport's runway, and then its terminals and interior.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Accidents and incidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Douala_International_Airport&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Accidents and incidents")]

* 4 March 1962: [Caledonian Airways Flight 153](/wiki/Caledonian_Airways_Flight_153 "Caledonian Airways Flight 153") – all 111 people on board died
* On 30 August 1984, **[Cameroon Airlines Flight 786](/wiki/Cameroon_Airlines_Flight_786 "Cameroon Airlines Flight 786")**, a [Boeing 737-200](/wiki/Boeing_737-200 "Boeing 737-200") registered as (TJ-CBD), experienced an engine malfunction when [taxiing](/wiki/Taxiing "Taxiing"). A fire from the damaged fuel tank engulfed the aircraft causing it to burn out. Two passengers were trapped by the flames and died. The remaining 107 passengers and seven crew members were able to evacuate the plane safely.[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* 3 December 1995: [Cameroon Airlines Flight 3701](/wiki/Cameroon_Airlines_Flight_3701 "Cameroon Airlines Flight 3701") – 71 out of 76 people on board died
* 5 May 2007: the [Kenya Airways Flight 507](/wiki/Kenya_Airways_Flight_507 "Kenya Airways Flight 507") scheduled for Abidjan – Douala – Nairobi crashed in [Mbanga Pongo](/w/index.php?title=Mbanga_Pongo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mbanga Pongo (page does not exist)") near Douala international airport, two minutes after it took off from the airport. Although the weather was bad, the report from the Cameroonian civil aviation authority said the pilots were to blame for the crash.[[21]](#cite_note-21) There were 114 fatalities, including 37 Cameroonians, 15 Indians and one American.[[22]](#cite_note-22)
